data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Organon & Co. misses bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
Kalshi's huge marketing blitz pays off with a Super Bowl win over online sportsbooks
data.description:
Market intelligence firm Sensor Tower reported that the 2026 Super Bowl highlighted the unprecedented growth the Kalshi (KALSHI) platform saw in 2025, largely due to its substantial advertising efforts coupled with its ability to compete with traditional sports betting apps. 

According to Sensor Tower estimates, the Kalshi mobile app saw its highest ever daily downloads in the app’s history during the Super Bowl weekend, peaking at ~150K downloads in the U.S. on the day before Super Bowl Sunday. That surge in downloads came at the expense of legacy sports betting platforms such as DraftKings (DKNG), FanDuel (FLUT), and BetMGM (MGM) (GMVHF), which all saw steep year-over-year declines for app downloads during Super Bowl weekend. Fanatics (FANA) was noted to be the only outlier of the cohort of sports betting platforms, while prediction betting platform Polymarket (POLYMARKET) followed Kalshi's path with a big spike in downloads.

 

 

"Kalshi’s ambitious marketing blitz in 2026 has resulted in the company ranking within the top 50 digital US advertisers by spend. In contrast, Kalshi ranked outside the top 40,000 advertisers during the same period in 2025," highlighted Sensor Tower. Notably, Kalshi (KALSHI) outspent Polymarket (POLYMARKE) in the U.S. by ~19X during the 2025 NFL season and outspent DraftKings (DKNG) Sportsbook by 35%.

data.title: Siemens rallies to record high on raised full-year outlook, quarterly earnings topper
data.description:
Siemens (SIEGY) +6.7% to a record high in Frankfurt trading Thursday after reporting better than expected FQ1 earnings and raising its profit guidance for FY 2026, boosted by surging demand for AI-driven data center infrastructure.

In the December quarter, Siemens (SIEGY) said net income fell to €2.22B from €3.87B in the year-earlier quarter, which benefited from a gain on the sale of Innomotics, but profit at its digital industries division jumped 15% to €2.9B, beating the €2.64B analyst consensus estimate, with a 17.8% profit margin, up from 14.5% in the prior-year period, driven by the automation and software businesses, which won several large contracts.

Group revenues rose 8% Y/Y to €19.14B and orders jumped 10% to €21.37B, beating the analyst consensus forecast of €19.10B and €20.84B, respectively, according to a company-compiled consensus; orders and revenues were up in all reporting regions, with strong comparable increases in both the U.S. and China, the company said.

Siemens (SIEGY) CEO Roland Busch said order growth was "supercharged" by large data center orders, mainly in the U.S. to build out cloud and AI infrastructure.

As a result, Siemens (SIEGY) raised its basic earnings outlook for its full year through September to €10.70-€11.10/share ($12.70-$13.18) from its previous guidance of €10.40-€11.00.

Siemens (SIEGY) was seeing moderate growth from the automotive industry, but momentum was building from defense, aerospace, and pharmaceuticals sector customers and machine builders, CFO Ralf Thomas said, according to Reuters.

However, investment sentiment remained "pretty shaky," Thomas said, with continuing conflict over tariffs and geopolitics providing limited visibility.

data.title: Qorvo shareholders overwhelmingly approve Skyworks merger, company says
data.description:
• Qorvo (QRVO) disclosed that its shareholders overwhelmingly approved its pending merger with Skyworks Solutions (SWKS).
  • More than 71.2M shareholders voted in favor of the deal, while just under 138,000 voted against, Qorvo said in a securities filing issued late on Wednesday. Just over 255,000 shareholders abstained. 
  • In October, Skyworks Solutions, which supplies radio frequency chips to Apple and other smartphone makers, agreed to buy Qorvo in a cash-and-stock transaction that values the combined enterprise at around $22B. The deal is expected to close by early 2027 following regulatory approval.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Qorvo shareholders will receive $32.50 in cash and 0.960 of a Skyworks common share for each Qorvo share held at the close of the transaction. Upon closing, Skyworks shareholders will own 63% of the combined company, while Qorvo shareholders will own 37%, on a fully diluted basis.
  • Both Skyworks and Qorvo disclosed earlier this month that they received a request for additional information from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission in connection with their deal.

data.title: Kelly Services Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 misses by $0.27, revenue of $1.05B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Kelly Services press release (KELYA): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 misses by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $1.05B (-11.8% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • First Quarter of 2026 – Expect Q1 to look similar to Q4, with revenue declining between 11% to 13% year-over-year, or between 3% to 5% on an underlying basis excluding discrete customer impacts, and adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 1.5%, which includes the impact of annual payroll tax resets. 
  • Remainder of Year – Assuming no new material impacts, expect relative improvement in year-over-year performance each successive quarter for both revenue and adjusted EBITDA margin resulting in modest year-over-year revenue growth and measurable adjusted EBITDA margin expansion in the second half of the year.
  • Shares -1.6% PM.
  •

data.title: PHINIA Non-GAAP EPS of $1.18 beats by $0.04, revenue of $889M beats by $50.73M
data.description:
• PHINIA press release (PHIN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.18 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $889M (+6.7% Y/Y) beats by $50.73M.
  • The Company expects 2026 net sales of $3.52 billion to $3.72 billion. This implies a year-over-year growth of 1% to 7% in 2026. The Company’s net earnings and adjusted EBITDA are projected to be $165 million to $195 million and $485 million to $525 million, respectively, with net earnings margin of 4.7% to 5.2% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.7% to 14.3%. The Company expects to generate $200 million to $240 million in adjusted free cash flow. Adjusted tax rate is expected to be in the range of 30% to 34%.

data.title: CME raises dividend by 4%
data.description:

data.title: McDonald’s wins value battle as Chipotle stumbles, Victoria Green says - CNBC interview
data.description:
McDonald’s (MCD) is emerging as a winner in the fast-food sector by leaning into value-oriented pricing and strategic promotions, according to investment expert Victoria Green of G Squared Private Wealth.

In an interview with CNBC, Green explained that McDonald’s deep understanding of its customer base is driving same-store sales growth while competitors like Chipotle (CMG) struggle to connect with value-conscious diners.

“What Chipotle is losing, McDonald’s gaining,” Green said during the interview, highlighting the contrast between the two chains’ approaches to pricing. She noted that while Chipotle has signaled it won’t offer dollar menu items, McDonald’s continues to attract customers with its $5 and $8 value meals and promotional offerings like the Snack Wrap.

Green praised the company’s operational excellence and franchisee support, describing McDonald’s leadership as particularly effective. “They’re great at promotion. They’re wonderful at supporting their franchisees. They’re building out. They’re growing same-store sales,” she explained. The analyst also pointed to the company’s ambitious expansion plans to 50,000 locations as evidence of its growth trajectory.

The investment strategist emphasized McDonald’s sophisticated approach to customer engagement through its app loyalty program. “They just know their consumers so well, so they’re able to price things well. They know when to hold them and know when to increase,” Green said. She described the company’s business structure, including its ownership of real estate, as “brilliant.”

Looking at the company’s product mix, Green noted that McDonald’s has achieved a roughly fifty-fifty balance between chicken and beef offerings. While she indicated her firm is watching margins carefully, she expects management to strike an upbeat tone given the strength of their consumer insights and operational execution.

Green recommended McDonald’s as a solid blue-chip core holding, comparing it favorably to Walmart ((WMT)) as a representation of the value consumer. The stock has demonstrated resilience despite weather-related headwinds in January, reflecting investor confidence in the company’s strategic direction.
